Hint.Alt: Alt + LMB: open the template settings.
Hint.Ctrl: Ctrl + LMB: copy the template as a custom template.

Compatibility.Title: Incompatible Properties found
Compatibility.Content: Properties "{q}" и "{f}" are mutually exclusive.<hr>Remove them?

Dialog.Name: Preset name
Dialog.Types: Determines which item types this template will be displayed for.

Names.Label: A list of random names
Names.Info:
A list of words or phrases that are randomly selected when an item is created.
A: Words or phrases before the item name.
B: Words or phrases after the item name.
C: Words or phrases completely replacing the item name.

Dialog.Info: Scripts that will be used by the item being created. When adding a script, you will be prompted to specify the chance of adding it during generation.
Dialog.Chance: Specify the chance of the script being triggered (%).

Create.Name: New script
Create.New: Specify the name of the new script
Create.Add: Add a new effect to an existing script

Hint.LMB: LMB: change the chance of adding a script.
Hint.RMB: RMB: remove the script from the template.

Dialog.Error: This script is already added to the template.

Text.Add: Select the scripts you want to add:
Text.Remove: Select the scripts you want to remove:

Labels.StartCombat: At the beginning of the fight
Labels.EndCombat: At the end of the fight

Error.ItemNotFound: The specified item was not found.
Error.PresetTypesEmpty: At least one item type must be selected.

Hereditary.Name: Hereditary
Hereditary.Title: This weapon has been passed down from father to son for generations. It is formidable and beautiful, but visibly worn.

TemplarWeapon.Name: Consecrated by the Templar
TemplarWeapon.Title: A weapon that has undergone a rigorous rite of purification in the great cathedral of Sigmar or Ulric. The item glows with righteous wrath and protects its bearer, but imposes a heavy spiritual and physical burden upon them.

TemplarArmour.Name: Consecrated by the Templar
TemplarArmour.Title: Heavy plate armour of the Witch Hunters or Templar Knights, consecrated by the high clergy. Holy litanies are engraved on the plates, warding off magic, yet the formidable weight of the armour imposes a physical burden on the warrior.

PunishingFlame.Name: Punishing Flame
PunishingFlame.Title: A weapon of the Witch Hunters, thoroughly imbued with the scent of soot, molten wax and fanatical faith. It strikes true against the enemies of mankind, but its furious aura consumes the strength of its wielder.

NulnExperiment.Name: Nuln Experiment
NulnExperiment.Title: A complex and cumbersome marvel of engineering from the Imperial School of Artillery. Equipped with pistons, gears and concealed valves. Extremely effective in battle, yet terrifyingly finicky to maintain.

Heroic.Name: Heroic
Heroic.Title: This item belonged to an ancient hero, whose essence is reflected in the properties of the artifact.

IthilmarWeapon.Name: Ithilmar
IthilmarWeapon.Title: A weapon forged from 'celestial silver'. It gleams brightly in the sun, weighs next to nothing, and seems to aid its wielder in striking down foes all on its own.

IthilmarArmor.Name: Ithilmar
IthilmarArmor.Title: An elegant suit of armour made of 'celestial silver', crafted by Elven artisans. It gleams brightly in the sun, weighs next to nothing, and does not hinder the wearer's ability to move stealthily.

Gromril.Name: Gromril
Gromril.Title: Gromril, also known as meteoric iron, star metal or silver steel, is a legendary metal — the epitome of durability and reliability. The finest weapons are forged from it, capable of serving for centuries without losing their properties. Unlike ordinary materials, Gromril is virtually eternal.

Mysterious.Name: Mysterious
Mysterious.Title: The item seems to possess some inner power. Or maybe not.

Magical.Name: Magical
Magical.Title: Magical winds have been bound into this item. It is skillfully crafted and imbued with power.

Ancient.Name: Ancient
Ancient.Title: This item reeks of history. It has been gathering dust for a long time, but it clearly has potential.

ThievingTrade.Name: Thieves'
ThievingTrade.Title: A worn and inconspicuous item with a secret compartment, crafted by masters from the criminal syndicates of Altdorf. Ideal for cowardly ambushes, but made of cheap, low-quality materials.

Rusted.Name: Rusted
Rusted.Title: This item has lain in dampness and filth for a long time. The metal parts are eaten away by corrosion, and the leather straps have rotted, so the whole structure may fall apart right in the middle of a battle.

WarpDistortedWeapon.Name: Warp-Distorted
WarpDistortedWeapon.Title: The item pulses with ominous unnatural energy. The shifting, chaotic geometry of the artefact makes it deadly, but highly unstable and repulsive to those around it.

WarpDistortedArmour.Name: Warp-Distorted
WarpDistortedArmour.Title: This armour is covered in ominous dark runes and thoroughly imbued with the corruption of Chaos. Its plates can fuse with the wearer’s body, protecting them from the fiercest attacks, but disfiguring their appearance.

Desecrated.Name: Desecrated
Desecrated.Title: This item was found in a place saturated with corruption. It can be dangerous both to enemies and to the owner themselves.

Damaged.Name: Damaged
Damaged.Title: This item looks damaged. It might not be wise to use it.

Fatal.Name: Fatal
Fatal.Title: This item has caused the death of many heroes, but every time there is someone who decides to acquire it.

Insignificant.Name: Insignificant
Insignificant.Title: This item is either someone's crude mockery or deliberate sabotage. Using it is sheer suicide.

Incendiary.Label: Incendiary
Incendiary.Hint: A weapon forged with Aqshy flares with searing flames upon being drawn, causing no harm to the wielder or their equipment. If the wielder deals damage to a target with this weapon, the target gains 1 Ablaze condition.

Elven.Label: Elven
Elven.Hint: These garments were crafted by Wood Elf mages and make the wearer far less conspicuous. Any Perception (sight‑based) or Ballistic Skill tests made against the wearer are performed with a −20 modifier

ObsidianInlays.Label: Obsidian Inlays
ObsidianInlays.Hint: This item is enchanted to disrupt hostile magic targeting the wearer. The wearer may attempt to dispel any spell directed at them (treat the wearer as having Language (Magic) 30 when attempting to dispel a spell).

Gromril.Label: Gromril
Gromril.Hint: Gromril is the hardest metal in the known world and one of the rarest. Gromril armor grants 1 extra point of armor (AP) and makes the wearer immune to critical hits, except when the character has already lost all their wounds. Gromril weapons gain the Unbreakable quality.
Gromril.Armour: The wearer of the Gromril armor is immune to critical injuries that affect the areas protected by the armor, except in cases where the character has already lost all their hit points.

Ithilmar.Label: Ithilmar
Ithilmar.Hint: Ithilmar means "heavenly silver," for it is as bright as the heavens and as shiny as polished silver. Ithilmar does not tarnish and is highly durable, rivaling the strength of the finest steel. The encumbrance of any Ithilmar item is reduced by 2 points, but not below 0.

Infected.Label: Infected
Infected.Hint: This weapon is a carrier of a dangerous infection. If a living target loses health as a result of an attack with this weapon, they must pass a Endurance test (+X) to prevent the wound from becoming infected.
Infected.Difficulty: Select the difficulty for testing Endurance

Poisoned.Label: Poisoned
Poisoned.Hint: This weapon is made of a poisonous material. If the target of the attack loses health, they are immediately poisoned. The difficulty of all tests related to this condition will be Challenging (+0).

HeavyArmor.Label: Heavy Armor
HeavyArmor.Hint: This armor has all the properties of heavy armor. It provides better protection, specifically 1 more armor points (AP), but it also encumbrance 1 more. Additionally, the wearer receives corresponding penalties to Stealth and Perception tests.

Throatseeker.Label: Throatseeker
Throatseeker.Hint: This magical weapon is made with an innate bloodlust, allowing it to pierce even the best defenses. It ignores any armor class provided by armor with Weak Points, but it cannot perform Undamaging attacks, and the flaw of the same name does not work.
Throatseeker.Undamaging: The "Undamaging" flaw was removed by adding the "Throatseeker" script.

TaintedWeapon.Label: Tainted Weapon
TaintedWeapon.Hint: These weapons have been polluted by the corrupting influence of Chaos. If the wielder damages the target with this weapon, it is exposed to a Weak Desecrating Effect. At the same time, in the event of a critical failure, the owner himself must withstand the same impact.

Message.NotOpposed: must perform a Average (+20) test against Minor @Corruption[minor]{corruption} exposure.

Quietened.Label: Quietened
Quietened.Hint: This piece of armor has been enchanted to remove the noise it makes when worn. The usual stealth penalty does not apply to her. However, the spells used for enchantment have not been perfected, and those wearing this armor can only speak in a whisper.

FullOfWind.Label: Full of Wind
FullOfWind.Hint: The winds of magic have a weak connection to this item. Each time it is used, the wearer must pass an Very Easy (+60) concentration test. In case of failure, roll on the weak magic recoil table. Characters without concentration skills automatically fail the test.

Draining.Label: Draining
Draining.Hint: The item draws strength from the owner's vital energy. Each time the item is used, the owner must perform a Average (+20) Endurance test. In case of failure, he gets a state of fatigue.

DrawingTheWinds.Label: Drawing the Winds
DrawingTheWinds.Hint: Magical currents swirl around the object. Anyone casting a spell within 10 yards of the wearer is exposed to The Swirling Winds.
DrawingTheWinds.Message: makes a roll on "The Swirling Winds" table and gets: {result}.

Cursed.Label: Cursed
Cursed.Hint: The item is cursed for betrayal. The spell cast on it is the opposite of the Ward trait. If the attack does not hit the owner of this item, roll 1d10. If you hit 7 or higher, the attack will hit the target with +1 SL, and another +1 SL for each number above 7. Many people don't realize that an object is cursed until they die from it.
Cursed.Message: Something strange prevented {defender} from defending against the attack!

RelentlessFury.Label: Relentless Fury
RelentlessFury.Hint: A gleaming weapon seems perfect at first glance. Every time it is used to deal damage, the character must pass a Challenging (+0) Willpower test. Moreover, each new test during a single battle receives a cumulative -10 modifier (up to a maximum of -30). If the test is failed, the character enters the Frenzy, gaining all its benefits. However, unlike regular Frenzy, it does not end when all enemies are defeated. Instead, the character is compelled to attack the nearest target, regardless of their identity. The Frenzy can only be interrupted by disarming, stunning, or incapacitating the character.
RelentlessFury.Message:
Must perform {difficulty} a Willpower test.
Upon failure, the character enters a Frenzy, receiving all its benefits, however, unlike normal Frenzy, it does not end when all enemies are defeated. On the contrary, the character must attack the nearest target, whoever it may be. The condition can only be interrupted by disarming, stunning, or disabling.

WeaponOfForsakenFortune.Label: Weapon of Forsaken Fortune
WeaponOfForsakenFortune.Hint: When creating this weapon, the most exotic materials were used: light shiny metal, bright white ivory and others. As long as the owner has at least 1 Fortune point, he gets +20 to the checks related to this weapon. However, every time an attack hits, but it has 2 SL or less, that is, it hits thanks to the bonus, it steals one point of Fortune.
WeaponOfForsakenFortune.NoFortune: {item} trying to steal a Fortune point from {actor}, but it doesn't find any!

Size&Species.Label: Size & Species
Size&Species.Hint: This item is custom-made, so its size may not be suitable for everyone. The master is free to determine for himself the effect that the use of an object unsuitable in size or belonging to a certain people will bring.

LootedItem.Label: Looted Item
LootedItem.Hint: This item was removed from the body of the murdered man, and only the gods know how shoddy it can be.
